import re, json, textwrap, sys, unicodedata

text = open("/mnt/data/file-GtY9KT9qzBGw4mMJmmpVRY-text_to_analyse.txt", 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# Find paraphrase "دعوة نوح لقومه كانت مدتها 950 سنة"
paraphrase_phrase = "دعوة نوح لقومه كانت مدتها 950 سنة"
paraphrase_match = re.search(re.escape(paraphrase_phrase), text)

paraphrase_span = None
if paraphrase_match:
    paraphrase_span = (paraphrase_match.start(), paraphrase_match.end())
paraphrase_span, text[paraphrase_span[0]:paraphrase_span[1]] if paraphrase_span else None

paren_texts = [(s+1, e, text[s+1:e]) for s,e in paren_spans]
paren_texts

dq_texts = [(s+1, e, text[s+1:e]) for s,e in dq_spans]
dq_texts

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ إِنِّي لَكُمْ نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ 11:25

output.code:
text = open("/mnt/data/file-GtY9KT9qzBGw4mMJmmpVRY-text_to_analyse.txt", 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# Build list of citation entries
citations = []

# 1) Paraphrase - Aya (Al-Ankabut 29:14)
paraphrase_phrase = "دعوة نوح لقومه كانت مدتها 950 سنة"
m = re.search(re.escape(paraphrase_phrase), text)
if m:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": m.start(),
        "span_end": m.end(),
        "span_text": paraphrase_phrase,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "العنكبوت", "sura_number": 29, "aya_number": 14},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

# 2) Parentheses segments - Aya
paren_spans = []
stack = []
for i, ch in enumerate(text):
    if ch == '(':
        stack.append(i)
    elif ch == ')':
        if stack:
            start = stack.pop()
            end = i
            paren_spans.append((start+1, end))  # exclude the parentheses

paren_texts = [(s, e, text[s:e]) for s,e in paren_spans]

# We'll map references based on proximity to preceding bracket labels if present.
# But since the text line shows bracket [هود: 23-24] after the parentheses, we can't easily parse to know them programmatically.
# We'll match the content to determine which is which.

for s, e, tseg in paren_texts:
    # Determine which label applies by looking at the following characters for surah info in square brackets []
    # Extract the substring from after ')' to next ']' within, say, 20 chars.
    following = text[e: e+50]
    # Extract bracket content [..] in following
    match_bracket = re.search(r'\[([^\]]+)\]', following)
    sura = None
    aya_ref = None
    if match_bracket:
        bracket = match_bracket.group(1)
        # bracket looks like "هود: 23-24" or "مريم: 98"
        # parse name till colon
        if ':' in bracket:
            parts = [p.strip() for p in bracket.split(':', 1)]
            sura_name = parts[0]
            aya_part = parts[1]
            # handle range or single
            if '-' in aya_part:
                a_start, a_end = [int(x.strip()) for x in aya_part.split('-')]
                # produce two entries for the range
                for aya_num in [a_start, a_end]:
                    citations.append({
                        "span_start": s,
                        "span_end": e,
                        "span_text": tseg,
                        "citation_type": "Aya",
                        "is_valid": False,
                        "aya_reference": {"sura_name": sura_name, "sura_number": 11 if sura_name == "هود" else (19 if sura_name == "مريم" else None), "aya_number": aya_num},
                        "hadeeth_reference": {},
                        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
                        "completely_invalid": True
                    })
            else:
                try:
                    aya_num = int(aya_part)
                except:
                    aya_num = None
                citations.append({
                    "span_start": s,
                    "span_end": e,
                    "span_text": tseg,
                    "citation_type": "Aya",
                    "is_valid": False,
                    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": sura_name, "sura_number": 11 if sura_name == "هود" else (19 if sura_name == "مريم" else None), "aya_number": aya_num},
                    "hadeeth_reference": {},
                    "corrected_text": "خطأ",
                    "completely_invalid": True
                })
        else:
            # bracket content without colon - ignore
            citations.append({
                "span_start": s,
                "span_end": e,
                "span_text": tseg,
                "citation_type": "Aya",
                "is_valid": False,
                "aya_reference": {"sura_name": None, "sura_number": None, "aya_number": None},
                "hadeeth_reference": {},
                "corrected_text": "خطأ",
                "completely_invalid": True
            })
    else:
        # no bracket found
        citations.append({
            "span_start": s,
            "span_end": e,
            "span_text": tseg,
            "citation_type": "Aya",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": {"sura_name": None, "sura_number": None, "aya_number": None},
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": "خطأ",
            "completely_invalid": True
        })

# 3) Double-quoted hadith segments
dq_spans = []
open_idx = None
for i,ch in enumerate(text):
    if ch == '"':
        if open_idx is None:
            open_idx = i
        else:
            dq_spans.append((open_idx+1, i))
            open_idx = None

for s, e in dq_spans:
    span = text[s:e]
    # Determine collection name and references by looking around the span within the text to find [البخاري: x/y] or [مسلم: a/b]
    # Search forward within 50 chars after the closing quote for [collection: book/hadith]
    following = text[e: e+60]
    mbr = re.search(r'\[([^\]:]+):\s*([0-9]+)\/([0-9]+)\]', following)
    collection_english = None
    book = None
    hadith_no = None
    if mbr:
        collection_ar = mbr.group(1).strip()
        book = int(mbr.group(2))
        hadith_no = int(mbr.group(3))
        collection_map = {"البخاري": "Sahih al-Bukhari", "مسلم": "Sahih Muslim"}
        collection_english = collection_map.get(collection_ar, collection_ar)
    citations.append({
        "span_start": s,
        "span_end": e,
        "span_text": span,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {
            "collection_name": collection_english if collection_english else "",
            "book_number": book if book is not None else None,
            "hadith_number": hadith_no if hadith_no is not None else None
        },
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

len(citations), citations[:2]

import json, sys, io, re
out = {"citations": citations}
json_str = json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False)
len(json_str)

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
